title: notopleura parasitica subsp. tetrapyrena
intro: Notopleura parasitica subsp. tetrapyrena is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae and is known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. In herbal medicine, Notopleura parasitica subsp. tetrapyrena is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting relaxation, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is often used in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments.
